content: Just as the vocabulary of reflection is taught as a core literacy skill in high school English to help students intentionally examine their own writing and thinking for growth, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be structured around explicit, literacy-aligned reflection routines modeled after K-12 language arts writing workshops, rather than only as ad-hoc technical AI debugging steps. This framing reframes LLM self-reflection as a teachable, iterative literacy skill rather than a purely computational optimization problem.
